text: We started the operation from the left side.
The patient positioned in right decubitus position and an intercostal uniportal incision were performed at the fifth intercostal space.
to which restrictions for the two lesions in the left lower lobe were performed using endoGIS tablets.
The frozen section pathology revealed the atypical adenomatous hyperplasia in both lesions.
After early test and hemostasis, chest drain was inserted in the same port and the wound was closed in layers.

the patient then turned to the left decubitus position while putting the chest drain between two pillows to prevent its occlusion a three centimeter incision was performed in the right fifth intercostal space then we started the resection of the superior segment of the right lower lobe by dissecting and opening the fissure above the pulmonary artery
then stapling the fissure using EnderGIS stapler.
the segmental bronchus to segment 6 were dissected encircled and divided using endo gis stapler
Now the venous branch to segment 6 was dissected, clipped and divided using energy device.
Two small arterial branches supplying segment 6 were found.
These branches were dissected, clipped and divided with energy device.
Two endogyary loads were needed to complete the resection and the segment was removed from the chest cavity.
Final pathology of this GGO was carcinoma in situ.
Post-operative course was uneventful and the patient was discharged home four days after the surgery.
